name: dudraw

metadata.name: dudraw
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A fork of Princeton's stddraw for use in classes at the University of Denver

documentation:
# dudraw

## Description

Repository for `dudraw`, a fork of [stddraw from the Princeton Standard Libraries](https://introcs.cs.princeton.edu/python/code/).

## Installation

```
pip install dudraw
```

## Documentation

API Documentation: [https://cs.du.edu/~intropython/dudraw/](https://cs.du.edu/~intropython/dudraw/)

To build documentation:

```
pip install pydoctor
make docs
```

## License

Released under the [GNU Public License version 3 (GPLv3)](https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.html)

## Authors

Original Authors of [StdLib](https://introcs.cs.princeton.edu/java/stdlib/):

    Robert Sedgewick
    Kevin Wayne
 
`dudraw` Fork Additions by:

    Joseph Brooksbank
    Ryan Dunagan
    Declan Kahn
    Eddy Rogers
    Andrew Hannum
    Mohammed Albow
    Jeff Edgington

modules.name: dudraw.color
documentation:
color.py

The color module defines the Color class and some popular Color
objects.

functions.name: dudraw.color._main
documentation: For testing:

classes.name: dudraw.color.Color

methods.name: dudraw.color.Color.__init__
documentation:
Construct self such that it has the given red (r),
green (g), blue (b), and alpha (a) components.
alpha (a) defaults to 255 which is non-transparent solid color.

methods.name: dudraw.color.Color.get_red
documentation: Return the red component of self.

methods.name: dudraw.color.Color.get_green
documentation: Return the green component of self.

methods.name: dudraw.color.Color.get_blue
documentation: Return the blue component of self.

methods.name: dudraw.color.Color.get_alpha
documentation: Return the alpha transparency component of self.

methods.name: dudraw.color.Color.__str__
documentation:
Return the string equivalent of self, that is, a
string of the form '(r, g, b)'.
A Color object models an RGB with Alpha Transparency color.

modules.name: dudraw.dudraw
documentation:
dudraw.py

The dudraw module defines functions that allow the user to create a
simple 2-dimensional drawing.  A drawing appears on the canvas.  The canvas appears
in the window.  As a convenience, the module also imports the
commonly used Color objects defined in the color module.

functions.name: dudraw.dudraw._pygame_color
documentation:
Convert c, an object of type Color, to an equivalent object
of type pygame.Color.  Return the result.
End added by Alan J. Broder

dudraw.dudraw.set_pen_width
documentation:
Set the pen width/radius to w 

This affects the subsequent drawing
of points and lines. If w is 0.0, then points will be drawn with
the minimum possible width and lines with the minimum possible
width.
@arg w: new value for the pen radius in pixels. This value must be non-negative, defaults to 0.0

@raises Exception: error thrown if the value of w is negative

documentation:

@return: the current value of pen color. This is an object of class Color

Save the window canvas as a .jpg to filepath specified.

@param filepath: the path of a folder to which the image will be saved

functions.name: dudraw.dudraw._show
documentation: Copy the canvas to the window canvas.

functions.name: dudraw.dudraw._show_and_wait_forever
documentation:
Copy the canvas to the window canvas. Then wait
forever, that is, until the user closes the dudraw window.

functions.name: dudraw.dudraw.show
documentation:
Copy the canvas to the window canvas, and
then wait for msec milliseconds. msec defaults to 0.0.

Note that everything we draw is on the canvas so won't show on the screen until we call show

@param msec: amount of milliseconds to wait after show, defaults to 0.0

functions.name: dudraw.dudraw._check_for_events
documentation:
Check if any new event has occured (such as a key typed or button
pressed).  If a key has been typed, then put that key in a queue.
